steps.title: Identify the extreme claim being illustrated
steps.content: The passage emphasizes the revisions are “extreme, even by James's standards.” The blank precedes a statement that critics regard the editions as different novels altogether. Both describe extremity—the transition must connect the severe revisions to the critical judgment that reflects this severity.

steps.title: Select the transition showing the critical judgment confirms the extreme claim
steps.content: ”In fact” shows the critics' assessment validates the characterization—their judgment proves just how extreme the revisions were. “By contrast,” “nevertheless,” and “in other words” misframe the relationship: they suggest opposition, concession, or equivalence when the two statements align in their emphasis on severity. This matches choice B — B is correct.
